_documentation: Registry of system-app permission exceptions. Each entry documents a permission
that is auto-granted (or otherwise resolved) for a system app outside the standard
user-grant flow.
Field names mirror freezr's existing permission record schema (see
middleware/permissions/permissionDefinitions.mjs) so the same vocabulary applies:
- requestor_app:    the app the grant is for
- type:             permission type from PERMISSION_DEFINITIONS (use_mail, use_llm, ...)
- name:             permission name (matches manifest, used for keying)
- granted, description:  pass through to the fabricated record
- PERMISSION_FIELD_EXCEPTIONS_BY_TYPE fields (connection_names, scopes, ...): typed defaults
- system_perm:      always true here; marker for audits
- reason:           human-readable rationale for the exception
How shortcuts are applied at runtime:
Middleware (llmContext.mjs, mailContext.mjs, ...) loads the user's granted perms
for (requestor_app, type) from the DB, then asks getSystemPermissionsFor to layer
any matching entries from this file on top. The fabricated record is in-memory only;
it is NOT written to the user's permissions DB. Removing an entry here disables
the shortcut immediately for all users — no DB cleanup needed.
Long-term: when the full system-app install pipeline lands
(see freezr_system_apps_design.md) this file becomes the source of truth for those
shortcuts, and the install pipeline reads it to know which permissions to auto-grant
vs. leave pending.

exceptions.name: mail_readwrite
exceptions.description: Built-in connections app reads and writes (send, mark-read, trash, delete) on any of the user's mail-enabled connections.
exceptions.reason: info.freezr.connections is the system viewer + workable client surfacing data the user manages at /account/resources. Write scope is needed for the Part-3 mail client (send / draft / mark-read / trash / delete). Per-connection write is still gated user-side by connection.access.mail === 'readwrite' — the system shortcut just removes the app-side grant dialog.

exceptions.name: contacts_readwrite
exceptions.description: Built-in connections app reads and writes contacts on any of the user's contacts-enabled connections.
exceptions.reason: Mirrors use_mail. Per-connection write is still gated user-side by connection.access.contacts === 'readwrite'.

exceptions.name: calendar_readwrite
exceptions.description: Built-in connections app reads and writes calendar events on any of the user's calendar-enabled connections.
exceptions.reason: Mirrors use_mail. Per-connection write is still gated user-side by connection.access.calendar === 'readwrite'.

exceptions.name: creator_llm
exceptions.description: Built-in creator app uses the user's LLM keys.
exceptions.reason: Legacy exception (predates the §2.4 principle). Consolidated here from a middleware-side `if (requestorApp === 'info.freezr.creator')` block. Behavior unchanged.

exceptions.name: admin_local_functions
exceptions.description: Admin app installs / removes / lists local (trusted) 3rd-party functions on the server.
exceptions.reason: info.freezr.admin/addlocalmicroservice.js calls freezr.serverless.upsertLocal / deleteLocal / getAllLocalFunctions. Pulls in the freezrApiV2.serverless.js add-on for admin pages.
